channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A long-lost ancient Roman artifact reappears in a New Orleans backyard
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A New Orleans family cleaning up their overgrown backyard made an extremely unusual find: Under the weeds was a mysterious marble tablet with Latin characters that included the phrase “spirits of the dead.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: Venezuelans in the U.S. are welcoming the news that opposition leader leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Price.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump is in 'exceptional health,' his doctor says, after visit to Walter Reed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is in “exceptional health," his physician said Friday after he underwent a checkup that included lab tests and preventive health assessments at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's physical results released, got COVID shot ahead of Middle East trip
item.description: Trump, 79, got flu and Covid shots at his evaluation, which comes ahead of a visit to the Middle East Monday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started, an attempt by President Donald Trump's administration to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown dragged into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday threatened to place an additional 100% tax on Chinese imports starting on Nov. 1 or sooner, potentially escalating tariff rates close to levels that in April fanned fears of a global recession.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started, an attempt by President Donald Trump's administration to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown dragged into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: John Lodge, singer and bassist of Moody Blues during classic era, dies at 82
item.description: LONDON (AP) — John Lodge, the singer and bassist of British rock band The Moody Blues for more than 50 years, has died. He was 82. In a statement Friday, his family said "with the deepest sadness” that Lodge died “suddenly and unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sam’s Club is changing its Sunday hours: What to know
item.description: Sam’s Club shoppers will soon have extended access to their preferred pallets of toilet paper and sacks of oranges.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man convicted of killing a 15-year-old girl in her home in 2001 is executed by injection in Indiana
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was executed by injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year. Roy Lee Ward, 53, was put to death at the Indiana State Prison in Michigan City.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Green group files Hatch Act complaints against HHS, Forest Service over 'partisan' shutdown messaging
item.description: An environmental advocacy group has filed complaints under the Hatch Act against the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and the Forest Service over what it described as “partisan” messaging blaming Democrats for the ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: A representative for the DHS provided a statement confirming that Kristi Noem’s remarks would be shown “in airports across the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Man sentenced for releasing noxious gas cloud at Meijer
item.description: A man will spend years in prison after a cloud of noxious gas was released during a theft at a grocery store in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators urge RFK Jr. to crack down on medication abortion
item.description: Almost every Senate Republican on Thursday urged the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to “reevaluate” whether a newly approved generic version of the abortion drug mifepristone should stay on the market. The letter to Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgement
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court on Thursday to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Rutgers professor signs petition to disband Turning Point USA from campus
item.description: A Rutgers professor signed a Change.org petition accusing the conservative Turning Point USA chapter of promoting hate speech and inciting violence against the community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Frustrations turn to heated confrontations as shutdown drags on
item.description: Partisan standoffs in the Capitol are bubbling into tense public confrontations as the government shutdown heads into its 10th day — with no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Drake's defamation lawsuit against label over Kendrick Lamar's 'Not Like Us' tossed out
item.description: In his lawsuit Drake asserted that the song amounts to “falsely accusing him of being a sex offender, engaging in pedophilic acts” and more.
item.dc:creator: Larry Newumeister, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, WGN 9

item.title: Senate passes mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The Senate approved its massive annual defense policy bill late Thursday as the U.S. government remains shut down. The GOP-led chamber approved the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) 70-20, aiming to fund the U.S. military at $924.7 billion in fiscal 2026.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon, journalists on collision course as restrictions on reporting loom
item.description: Facing a deadline next week on whether to sign a statement acknowledging new restrictions on how they do their jobs or risk being thrown out of the Pentagon, journalists who cover the U.S. military appear headed toward a showdown with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4
